mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Option skip-provider-button provides white page with "Found." link
mission.description: Option skip-provider-button provides white page with "Found." link. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
When I use the parameter -skip-provider-button, the login button is not shown anymore, but a white page with a single link named "Found" instead.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Option skip-provider-button provides white page with…
steps.description:
It's a simple workaround that wraps the original `http.Redirect' method.
```yaml
oauth2:
  image: oauth2_proxy # build from repository (armv6)
  command:
    - -cookie-domain=xxx
    - -cookie-secure=true
    - -cookie-secret=xxx
    - -email-domain=*
    - -http-address=0.0.0.0:4180
    - -pass-access-token
    - -provider=gitlab
    - -gitlab-group=xxx
    - -client-id=xxx
    - -client-secret=xxx
    - -set-authorization-header
    - -set-xauthrequest
    - -whitelist-domain=.${DOMAIN}
    - -footer=-
    - -banner=xxx
    - -provider-display-name=me
    - -skip-provider-button=true
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Option skip-provider-button provides white page with…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: It's a simple workaround that wraps the original `http.Redirect' method.
codeSnippets:
oauth2:
  image: oauth2_proxy # build from repository (armv6)
  command:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
